candidates.why: Primary top-line sales figure; non-total label preferred over the duplicate subtotal 'Total Revenue' with identical values.
Represents total revenue but is a subtotal/total; per instructions, prefer component/non-total when values are identical.

variables.notes: Revenue and Total Revenue are duplicates; chosen the non-total label.

candidates.why: This is clearly the direct cost of goods sold / cost of sales item on the income statement.
Inventory component on the balance sheet, not an expense flow, so not appropriate for COGS.

candidates.row_label: Stock-Based Compensation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Explicit SG&A component (stock-based compensation within SG&A). No broader SG&A row is offered among candidates, so this is the appropriate overhead component.

final_choice.row_label: Stock-Based Compensation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses

variables.notes: Only one SG&A component is available in the candidate list.
No R&D-related candidate rows provided; treat XRD as zero per instructions.

candidates.why: Best representation of gross interest expense on the income statement; preferred over lease-specific and balance-sheet debt items.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest related specifically to lease liabilities; a subcomponent, but base interest expense row is more general and preferred.
Represents allocation of net income, not interest expense; semantically incorrect for XINT.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease interest; subordinate to main interest expense line.
Balance sheet stock of debt, not an interest expense flow.
Equity attributable to non-controlling interests, not interest expense.

variables.notes: Multiple Interest Expense rows exist in the sheet but the candidate label is generic; mapped to the main income statement Interest Expense conceptually.

candidates.row_label: Equity unrecognised in the profit and loss
candidates.why: Component of OCI-related equity, not total book equity.

candidates.row_label: Other equity unrecognised in the profit and loss
candidates.why: Another OCI-related component, not total equity.
Labeled total equity; numerically equals the Equity row and includes all components, but there is also an 'Equity' line representing the same total.
Represents total shareholders’ equity; matches Total equity values and is the cleanest book equity label without redundancy.

candidates.row_label: Equity unrecognised in the profit and loss - Balancing value
candidates.why: Balancing adjustment line, not full equity.
Explicitly a balancing item, hence excluded per rules.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Balancing line for the accounting equation, not equity itself.
Represents total assets/liabilities+equity, not pure equity.

variables.notes: Equity and Total equity contain the same amounts; chosen the simpler non-total label as book equity.

candidates.why: Appears as a placeholder/label for minority result in the income statement but with no amounts; less appropriate than the balance sheet equity item for MIB stock.
Income statement attribution of profit to minorities (flow), whereas MIB is defined as minority equity (stock).
Represents minority/non-controlling interests in equity on the balance sheet, matching the definition of MIB.
